title: [Bug]: release validation CI fails with latest Ruff lint rules
summary: The v0.4.2 release-validation CI run failed in the lint step because the hosted runner installed a newer Ruff version than the local environment and surfaced stricter lint findings.
observed: Release-validation run 26654606716 failed `ruff check .` on all Python matrix jobs. The log reported two unused `noqa: S603` directives in local container e2e helper scripts and one ASYNC240 warning for calling a pathlib method directly inside an async multi-sink routing helper.
expected: Release validation should pass with the current CI-resolved Ruff version, and local regression coverage should prevent stale suppressions or async pathlib calls from returning.
reproduction: Run the release-validation CI workflow for PR #330 or run `ruff check .` with the CI-resolved Ruff version.
failing_test: Add a focused static regression test that detects bare stale S603 suppressions in the container e2e helpers and direct `Path.mkdir` use inside the async multi-sink routing flow.
impact: The release is blocked until the lint findings are fixed. Runtime sink delivery semantics are unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to CI lint compatibility and local test helper structure. It cannot alter JetStream ACK, retry, DLQ, or durable sink commit behavior.
security: The fix must not weaken subprocess safety. Container e2e helpers must continue to use fixed argv lists, `shell=False`, bounded timeouts, and explicit environments.
acceptance: The stale bare S603 suppressions are replaced with version-compatible suppressions without changing safe subprocess argument-list behavior.
The async multi-sink routing helper no longer calls pathlib methods directly from async code.
A focused static regression covers the lint compatibility expectations.
The release-validation CI workflow passes after the fix.
The latest test report and changelog record the release-blocking CI fix.
tests: Run the focused static regression, `python -m ruff check .`, `scripts/check.sh`, and the release-validation GitHub workflow for PR #330.
documentation: Update the latest test report and changelog. Public runtime documentation does not need a behavior change note because the fix is CI and helper-structure only.
closeout: Close after the fix merges into the release branch, release validation passes, and release-gated closure confirms v0.4.2 has shipped.
